reasoning: The Lean signature defines S as the set of reals satisfying x^4 + 36 ≤ 13x^2 and f as x ↦ x^3 - 3x, matching the informal statement exactly. `IsGreatest {f x | x ∈ S} putnam_1986_a1_solution` asserts that the solution value is in the image of S under f (so it is attained) and is an upper bound for that image — this is precisely the standard definition of "the maximum value of f on S". The set-builder notation `{f x | x ∈ S}` denotes the image f(S), as required. The formalization captures both the attainment and upper-bound aspects of "maximum" correctly and introduces no extra or missing hypotheses.
